title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo's mother, affected by Alzheimer's, inspired her participation in the marathon.
Memories of her mother's cooking evoke strong emotions and motivate her fundraising efforts.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to Nashville, bringing their road record to 11-14-4.
The Bruins are currently the second Wild Card team in the Eastern Conference.
General Manager Don Sweeney considers trade options as Bruins approach the NHL trade deadline.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: U.S. Court of Appeals Judge David Barron grants Seamus Culleton's stay of removal pending review resolution.
Seamus Culleton, detained in Texas, overstayed a U.S. visa in 2009; facing deportation despite a pending green card.
Culleton's lawyer claims he has no U.S. criminal record; Culleton opted for detention over immediate deportation.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a house fire early Tuesday morning in Foxborough.
The fire broke out at about 3 a.m. on Granite Street, with flames already venting through the windows.
The cause of the fire is under investigation after the Foxborough Fire Department extinguished the blaze with aid from Mansfield and Plainville.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree coverage expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, reaching 28.5 percent citywide.
The expansion was driven by growth in public parks and rights-of-way, particularly in East Boston and Jamaica Plain.
The tree canopy increase aligns with Boston's Urban Forest Plan and broader climate goals, focusing on emissions reduction and environmental risk.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Kraft Group pledges help covering $7.8 million in World Cup security costs at Gillette Stadium, but Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied.
Foxborough's Select Board demands clearer commitments by March 17 to secure safety funding for seven World Cup games.
BS26 promises quick reimbursement for security expenses, but skepticism over group's financial capacity persists among town leaders.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing after nearly a decade in business, citing high insurance costs.
The bar has been a popular spot for Northeastern University students, located on Gainsborough Street.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock stated liquor liability insurance costs have risen from $18,000 to over $75,000 annually.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return for the Boston Celtics against the Dallas Mavericks after a 10-month Achilles injury recovery.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla must adjust the team's rotation, potentially placing Baylor Scheierman or Sam Hauser on the bench.
Tatum's return could reduce playtime for younger players like Baylor Scheierman and Hugo Gonzalez, affecting team dynamics.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's Achilles' tendon ruptured during Game 4 of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Knicks, sidelining him for nearly 10 months.
Tatum underwent surgery on May 13, 2025, and began a structured recovery process, including public appearances and practice sessions with the Celtics.
The Celtics upgraded Tatum's injury status to questionable for a game against the Mavericks, indicating his anticipated return to play this week.

title: Choosing which of my two cars to keep
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA's Car Doctor advises keeping the 2007 Honda Element for better safety and performance over the 2005 model.
Mechanic checks on rust, leaks, suspension, and brakes are recommended to decide between two aged vehicles.
Honda CR-V spark plugs typically last 100,000 miles; replacement not urgent unless car shows issues like rough idling.
